patches.find: The dome needs a base disc radius, an exponential height function, an ellipse axis ratio, and a longitude scale factor, and still reports 5.2% mean error
patches.replace: The dome needs at least four free parameters (H₀ = 8,537 km, λ = 8,619 km, EW angular scale 0.9941, and r_eq = 14,105 km) and still reports 5.2% mean error
patches.rationale: Scale comparison paragraph still named parameters as 'ellipse axis ratio' while the V50.6 paragraph (patched in prior cycle) correctly names r_eq = 14,105 km. Internal self-contradiction within one WIN. Also restructures to count actual free parameters consistently, matching the V50.6 paragraph's style. Curmudgeon c5 review (WIN-027.c5.json) moderate hole.

patches.replace: globe geodetic algorithm achieves sub-millimetre precision
patches.rationale: Three-layer precision inconsistency: finding says '<0.5% error', tldr says 'sub-meter', detail implies 'sub-millimetre' (Karney 2013). Aligning finding to the strongest honest claim (sub-millimetre algorithmic precision per Karney 2013) makes the dome's 5.2% error look worst by comparison. Curmudgeon c5 review minor hole.
Aligning tldr_evidence to match the corrected finding (sub-millimetre per Karney 2013). Consistent claim across finding and tldr strengthens the argument.

patches.find: publicly available well before the dome incorporated it
patches.replace: publicly available years before the dome incorporated it (Kim & Chang published in 2018; the dome incorporated it only in V51.0, early 2026 — a nine-year gap)
patches.rationale: Vague 'well before' replaced with specific timeline: 2018 paper publication vs V51.0 (early 2026). Nine-year gap is a stronger post-hoc argument than an unquantified 'well before'. ISS-1041.

patches.find: Note: WIN-010, WIN-025, and WIN-068 all invoke the same ionospheric Sq mechanism
patches.replace: The dome's refraction cascade (see Section 1.8) further undermines the FSF's geometric derivation claim — if the dome's own n(r) medium makes the sun's physical position seasonally indeterminate, the FSF ratio anchored to BOU dome-coordinate geometry inherits that indeterminacy. Note: WIN-010, WIN-025, and WIN-068 all invoke the same ionospheric Sq mechanism
patches.rationale: ISS-1042: WIN-010 and WIN-025 should match WIN-068's refraction cascade cross-reference to Section 1.8 for consistency. The cascade argument (seasonal indeterminacy of sun position via n(r)) applies equally to all three eclipse WIN claims that use FSF. Adding the Section 1.8 cross-reference before the Note paragraph.

patches.find: it relabels a 90-year-old standard prediction as 'aetheric.'
patches.replace: it relabels a 90-year-old standard prediction as ‘aetheric.’ The dome's refraction cascade further undermines the FSF derivation claim: if the dome's own n(r) medium makes the sun's physical position seasonally indeterminate, any FSF ratio anchored to dome-coordinate geometry inherits that indeterminacy (see Section 1.8). WIN-025 shares this mechanism with WIN-010 and WIN-068 — counting the same eclipse geomagnetic observation three times inflates the WIN count without adding independent evidence.
patches.rationale: ISS-1042: WIN-025 lacked both the Section 1.8 cross-reference and the mechanism-sharing/count-inflation note that WIN-010 and WIN-068 carry. Adding both for consistency.
